pakistani|devon-ave: Devon Avenue in Chicago serves as the heart of the city's South Asian community, earning the nickname "Little India" for its authentic concentration of Pakistani, Indian, and Bangladeshi businesses stretching along a vibrant two-mile corridor. From late-night karahi spots to family-run establishments serving homestyle biryanis, Devon Avenue provides an unmatched halal dining experience that draws food lovers from across the Midwest.

The city's Pakistani restaurants are known for their generous portions, bold spice blends, and commitment to halal preparation methods that cater to the local Muslim community and food enthusiasts alike.
